Article.ArticleTitle: The combination of ozone and Mn(II) for efficient removal of ozone-resistant N, N-diethyl-3-toluamide.

Abstract.AbstractText: This study investigates the enhancement of ozonation efficiency through the introduction of manganese ions (O3/Mn(II)) that significantly improve the degradation of ozone-persistent organic pollutants. Manganese ions accelerate hydroxyl radical generation by catalyzing ozone decomposition, thereby facilitating more effective oxidative reactions. The application of O3/Mn(II) to degrade N, N-Diethyl-3-toluamide (DEET) achieved a decomposition efficiency of 3.6 to 4.1 times higher than ozonation alone. The promotive effect was highly pH-dependent, with the observed rate constant (kobs) for O3/Mn(II) reaching 97.05 M-1s-1 at pH 4, approximately 3.3 times greater than ozonation alone, and 686.93 M-1s-1 at pH 7, around 1.7 times higher. O3 oxidation contributed approximately 1 % to the overall process, while hydroxyl radicals accounted for approximately 80 %, and manganese species accounted for approximately 20 %. Furthermore, the oxidation state of manganese significantly influenced the cooperative oxidation efficiency, with Mn(VII) achieving the highest kobs (680.87 M-1s-1), while Mn(IV) exhibited the lowest (304.6 M-1s-1). Additionally, intermediate products formed during DEET oxidation were identified using Orbitrap technology, elucidating various degradation pathways (such as decarboxylation, tri-hydration, and aromatic ring opening). These findings provide valuable insights into enhancing ozonation processes for environmental remediation.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Declaration of competing interest The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.
